rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aziridination of triaryl-substituted alkenes using hexafluoroisopropanol sulfamate (HfsNH2) as a nucleophilic nitrogen source, proceeding via stepwise anodic oxidations and two C-N bond formations
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the alkene to a carbon radical cation; a second anodic oxidation of the neutral C-centered radical (after sulfamate addition/deprotonation) to a carbocation; cathodic reduction of protons (assisted by lutidine/LiClO4/MeCN) releases H2

key_intermediates.name: neutral β-sulfamyl C-centered radical (B) after nucleophilic addition of HfsNH2 and deprotonation by lutidine
β-sulfamyl carbocation (C) from second anodic oxidation
aziridine product from intramolecular C-N ring closure
Ritter-type nitrilium / dihydroimidazole (7a, 7b-7d) from MeCN trapping of cation (off-cycle trap)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the triarylalkene to a carbon radical cation (A); sulfamate nitrogen attacks and lutidine deprotonates to give a neutral β-amido C-radical (B); a second anodic SET oxidizes B to a carbocation (C), which undergoes intramolecular nucleophilic ring closure by the sulfamate nitrogen to deliver the aziridine, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: (E)- and (Z)-stilbene (6a)
probe_reagents.observation: Cation generated under the conditions is trapped by MeCN solvent to give the same trans-dihydroimidazole (Ritter-type) 7a in 50%/47% from E/Z isomers, supporting a planar carbocation intermediate

probe_reagents.name: 4-Br, 4-Cl, 4-F stilbenes (6b-6d)
probe_reagents.observation: Also give MeCN-trapped dihydroimidazoles 7b-7d (37-44%), confirming generality of carbocation intermediate

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, lutidine, LiClO4
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation wave at ~+2.0 V vs SCE matches the oxidation potential of alkene 1a alone, indicating SET to anode occurs from the alkene, not from sulfamate

probe_reagents.name: CuI / Rh2(OAc)4
probe_reagents.observation: Nitrene-pathway metal catalysts gave no or only trace aziridine, arguing against a nitrene mechanism

notes: Authors explicitly contrast their alkene-activation pathway with the conventional nitrene/nitrogen-radical pathway (Scheme 1a vs 1b); the failure of CuI and Rh2(OAc)4 nitrene catalysts and the trapping of a planar carbocation (loss of E/Z stereochemistry in stilbene→7a) disfavor a nitrene mechanism. LiClO4-activated 2,6-lutidine or MeCN are proposed to participate in cathodic discharge.
